message:
fix(ondisk,extent,repair): add checksum stamping functions and harden integer casts

Add stamp_dir_block_checksum() and stamp_extent_block_checksum() to
ffs-ondisk for writing CRC32C checksums into directory and extent
tree block tails — the write-path counterparts to the existing
verify_* functions needed for directory/extent mutation operations.

Replace unsafe `as u32` casts throughout ffs-extent with explicit
u32::try_from().expect() calls that document the invariant being
relied upon, preventing silent truncation if values ever exceed u32
range. Apply the same pattern to ffs-mvcc WAL writer's append count.

Additional fixes:
- Add #[must_use] to trim_extents, hit_rate, ExtentCache::new/with_capacity
- Fix clippy too_many_lines lint in append_commit with #[expect]
- Replace unnecessary &mut with & for stream in configure_stream_timeouts
- Remove unnecessary .clone() on seed_symbols in exchange test
- Use checked_sub for Instant arithmetic in pipeline tests
- Use inline format args in autopilot test assertions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
